utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced structurally complete reports passing all verifier checks. However, wi_skills produces a higher-quality analysis for investment committee use. Key differentiators: (1) wi_skills explicitly critiques the '$50B+' TAM claim by name with a direct numeric comparison, while wo_skills only addresses the vague 'massive' claim without citing the specific figure; (2) wi_skills uses a cleaner three-methodology triangulation (top-down, bottom-up, value-based) with explicit reconciliation, whereas wo_skills primarily relies on bottom-up with looser top-down validation; (3) wi_skills produces a single deterministic TAM/SAM/SOM figure rather than ranges, making committee evaluation more straightforward; (4) wi_skills SOM is set at 10% which slightly exceeds the 2-5% new-entrant benchmark without sufficient justification, which is a weakness shared implicitly by wo_skills as well. Overall, wi_skills delivers more structured, rigorous, and immediately usable analysis as intended by the skill design, while wo_skills—despite performing web searches—produces a report with wider ranges and less precise framing of the founder's claim credibility.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both completed successfully and produced output files.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both runs produced high-quality, value-theory-grounded market sizing analyses that largely exceed the expected baseline behavior. Contrary to the test hypothesis, the wo_skills agent also correctly avoided generic cloud market figures and built from customer value data. However, wi_skills demonstrates marginal advantages: (1) it explicitly invokes the market-sizing-analysis skill, providing a structured methodology framework; (2) it produces a more comprehensive output (11,437 bytes vs. 7,514 bytes) with deeper customer segmentation tables; (3) its TAM calculation uses the full 12,000-company universe before narrowing, which is methodologically cleaner. The key differentiator where wi_skills fails is JE6: it sets SOM upper bound at 15%, exceeding the ≤10% threshold. Meanwhile wo_skills caps at exactly 10% with explicit new-category justification, passing JE6. On JE2, wi_skills more clearly applies a WTP percentage fraction to customer value, while wo_skills conflates ACV with value. Overall, wi_skills scores 6/7 vs wo_skills 6/7 on these criteria — comparable quality — but wi_skills produces a structurally richer, more complete analysis. The gap between wi_skills and wo_skills is smaller than expected because the wo_skills agent independently adopted value-based methodology from the founder context document.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive market sizing analyses that pass all seven evaluation criteria. Both outputs: (1) construct per-segment bottom-up TAMs across all three customer groups, (2) apply the 0.8% take rate formula, (3) use the 55% geographic filter explicitly, (4) ground SOM in competitive benchmark data, (5) apply the 19% CAGR quantitatively, (6) prioritize segments with data, and (7) address dual audiences. The wi_skills output is marginally more detailed (11,550 bytes vs. 7,822 bytes) and includes richer narrative context and a more structured banking partner section. The wo_skills output is more concise but equally rigorous analytically. The key difference is that the skill invocation in wi_skills produced a slightly larger, more elaborately structured document with additional sections like a dedicated 'Banking Partner Value Proposition.' Overall, both are effectively equivalent in meeting the evaluation criteria, with wi_skills having a slight edge in presentation richness.

utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective than wo_skills. Both runs produce solid bottom-up TAM construction (JE1), SAM derivation with multiple filters (JE2), and realistic SOM (JE3), with assumption documentation (JE6). The wo_skills run deliberately avoided the top-down approach and never referenced the $50B figure, resulting in a report that lacks two critical elements the investment committee needs for independent deal evaluation. The wi_skills report also passes 3 out of 4 verifier checks vs only 1 for wo_skills, confirming substantially higher structural completeness. Conclusion: wi_skills is materially more effective for this market sizing task.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive market sizing analyses that satisfy all seven evaluation criteria. Both correctly implement the bottom-up multi-segment TAM, apply the 55% geographic filter for SAM, anchor SOM to competitive benchmarks, project 5-year growth using the 19% CAGR, prioritize segments with quantitative backing, and present the analysis for a dual audience. The wi_skills output is marginally more structured (explicitly invoking the /market-sizing-analysis skill, using a cleaner format with a dedicated 'Methodology' section, and a more concise 'Board Takeaway'), while wo_skills produces a slightly longer document with more detailed narrative sections. The analytical quality and completeness are essentially equivalent across all evaluated dimensions. The one differentiator is that wi_skills explicitly references the skill invocation as required by the instruction, and the wo_skills output failed one verifier check (VC4), though this appears to be a parsing artifact rather than a real content gap. Overall, wi_skills is marginally more effective due to its cleaner structure and compliance with the skill invocation requirement, but the substantive analytical quality is nearly identical.
